landing.title: Are you a good person?
landing.label: Examination
landing.subtitle: Ten commandments. Eight questions. One verdict.

verdict.deathLineTemplate: {count} people died while you answered these questions.
verdict.confessionBoth: A {admitted} — by your own admission. And a {denied} — by your evasions.

questions.text: Have you ever told a lie — even a small one?
questions.commandment: You shall not bear false witness
questions.followUp: What about a 'white lie' to spare someone's feelings?

questions.text: Have you ever taken something that wasn't yours — even something small?
questions.followUp: Have you ever kept extra change, or downloaded something you didn't pay for?

questions.text: Have you ever looked at someone with lust?
questions.followUp: Jesus said, 'Whoever looks at a woman to lust for her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.' — Matthew 5:28 (NKJV)

questions.text: Have you ever been so angry you wished someone harm?
questions.followUp: Even a flash of hatred — Jesus said, 'Whoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment.' — Matthew 5:22 (NKJV)

questions.text: Have you ever used God's name as a curse word?
questions.commandment: You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain
questions.followUp: Even 'OMG' as an exclamation — using the Creator's name carelessly?

questions.text: Have you ever wanted something that belonged to someone else?
questions.followUp: A friend's lifestyle, someone's relationship, a coworker's promotion?

questions.text: Have you ever put something — money, career, relationships — above God?
questions.commandment: You shall have no other gods before Me
questions.followUp: If you've spent more time on your phone than thinking about your Creator...

questions.text: Have you ever failed to honor your parents?
questions.commandment: Honor your father and your mother
questions.followUp: Even in your heart — resentment, dismissiveness, ingratitude?

verdict.title: Guilty.
verdict.subtitle: By God's perfect standard, none of us are good enough.

grace.heading: But God, being rich in mercy...
grace.body:
Imagine you're in a courtroom, guilty of a serious crime. The fine is enormous — more than you could ever pay. But then someone steps in and pays your fine in full. The judge can legally let you go — justice has been served.

That's what God did for you.

Two thousand years ago, God came to earth in the person of Jesus Christ. He lived a perfect life — the only person who ever could. Then He willingly died on the cross, taking the punishment that we deserve.

He paid the fine so that God, the just Judge, could dismiss your case. Not because you earned it. Not because you deserved it. But because He loves you.

Three days later, He rose from the dead, defeating death itself.
grace.scripture: For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have everlasting life.
grace.label: Grace

invitation.heading: What will you do with this?
invitation.prayer: God, I understand that I have broken Your Law and sinned against You. I am truly sorry. I believe that Jesus Christ died on the cross to pay the penalty for my sins and that He rose again. I turn from my sins and place my trust in Jesus Christ alone for my salvation. Thank You for Your mercy and forgiveness. In Jesus' name, Amen.

responses.thinking: I want to think about it

share.telegramMessage: Are you a good person? Take this test and find out:

meta.title: Are You a Good Person? | Take the Test
meta.description: Most people think they're good. Find out where you really stand.

quiz.heading: Are you ready to face God?
quiz.subtitle: Answer honestly.

questions.question: Have you ever told a lie?
questions.yesResponse: Even one lie. A "white lie," an exaggeration, a half-truth.
questions.noResponse: Really? You've never told a single lie in your entire life? That itself might be one.

questions.question: Have you ever stolen anything?
questions.yesResponse: Regardless of the value — a pen, someone's time, credit for someone else's work.
questions.noResponse: Never taken anything that wasn't yours? Not even something small? Think again.

questions.question: Have you ever used God's name as a curse word?
questions.yesResponse: Instead of using a four-letter word, you used the name of the God who gave you life.
questions.noResponse: Perhaps not — but by two of the Ten Commandments, you've already admitted guilt.

quiz.guiltyDesc: By your own admission, you are a lying, thieving blasphemer.
quiz.scripture: For whoever keeps the whole law and yet stumbles at just one point is guilty of breaking all of it.
quiz.deathsDuring: {count} people died while you answered these questions.

grace.heading: But God...
grace.body1: You're guilty. The death counter keeps ticking — and one of those numbers will be yours. But here is the good news: God became a man — Jesus Christ — lived a perfect life, and willingly died on the cross to pay the fine for your sins. Then He rose from the dead, defeating death itself.
grace.body2: If you repent (turn from sin) and trust in Jesus Christ, God will forgive every sin you've ever committed and grant you eternal life. That's not earned — it's a free gift.
grace.scripture: But God demonstrates His own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

resources.name: Are You a Good Person? — Living Waters

landing.title: Have a conversation
landing.subtitle: An honest talk about life, morality, and what comes after.

systemPrompts.intro:
You are a warm, thoughtful person having a genuine conversation about morality and faith. Your approach is inspired by Ray Comfort's street evangelism — gentle, curious, and question-driven.

Start by warmly greeting the person and asking: 'Do you think you're a good person?'

Keep your opening short — one or two sentences max. Be warm and casual, like you're chatting on the street.

Respond in English. Use the NKJV Bible version for any Scripture references.
systemPrompts.commandments:
You are continuing a conversation about morality. The person has shared their view on being a good person.

Your goal: Walk them through the Ten Commandments naturally. Ask if they've ever:
- Told a lie (9th commandment)
- Stolen anything, even small (8th commandment)
- Looked at someone with lust (7th — Jesus said this is adultery of the heart, Matthew 5:28)
- Been angry enough to wish someone harm (6th — Jesus said this is murder of the heart, Matthew 5:22)
- Used God's name as a curse word (3rd commandment)
- Coveted what belongs to others (10th commandment)

Do NOT list all commandments at once. Weave them naturally into the conversation, one or two at a time. If they deny something, gently probe with real-world examples (like Ray Comfort does).

After covering at least 4 commandments, call the advance_stage tool.

Respond in English. Use NKJV for Scripture.
systemPrompts.conviction:
You are helping someone see the weight of what they've admitted in the conversation.

Summarize what they confessed — they admitted to lying (making them a liar), stealing (a thief), lusting (an adulterer at heart), etc. Use their own words where possible.

Then ask: 'If God judged you by the Ten Commandments on Judgment Day, would you be innocent or guilty?'

Let them answer. If they say guilty, acknowledge the weight of that. If they resist, gently remind them of what they admitted.

Do NOT present the gospel yet. Stay in conviction. Once they've acknowledged guilt or you've clearly presented the verdict, call advance_stage.

Respond in English. Use NKJV for Scripture.
systemPrompts.grace:
You are now sharing the gospel — the good news — with someone who has acknowledged they've broken God's law.

Shift your tone to warm and hopeful. Use the courtroom analogy:
- They're guilty before a just Judge
- The fine is enormous — more than they could ever pay
- But someone stepped in and paid their fine in full
- That someone is Jesus Christ

Explain: God came to earth as Jesus Christ, lived a perfect life, and willingly died on the cross to take the punishment we deserve. He paid the fine so God could legally dismiss our case — not because we earned it, but because He loves us. Three days later, He rose from the dead.

Quote John 3:16 (NKJV): 'For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have everlasting life.'

After presenting the full gospel, call advance_stage.

Respond in English. Use NKJV for Scripture.

meta.title: Have an Honest Conversation | Good Person Test
meta.description: A personal conversation about life, morality, and what comes after.
